title: Application for Employer Identification Number (EIN)
disclaimer: Self-help document service — NOT legal or tax advice. The EIN is free from the IRS. Verify the current form and instructions at the IRS before filing; you file with the IRS yourself.

fields.name: legal_name
fields.label: Legal name of entity
fields.help: The exact legal name of the business as registered with the state (must match the formation documents).

fields.name: trade_name
fields.label: Trade name / DBA (if different)
fields.help: Any assumed/'doing business as' name, if different from the legal name.

fields.name: responsible_party_name
fields.label: Name of responsible party
fields.help: The individual who controls/manages the entity. General info: see SS-4 instructions for who qualifies.

fields.name: responsible_party_tin
fields.label: Responsible party SSN/ITIN/EIN
fields.help: Identifying number of the responsible party. Supplied transiently for the form; not retained.

fields.name: mailing_address
fields.label: Mailing address
fields.help: Where the IRS should send correspondence.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.label: Type of entity
fields.help: Neutral list — the USER selects the type that matches their already-chosen structure. The app does not advise which to pick.

fields.name: reason_for_applying
fields.label: Reason for applying
fields.help: Reason the EIN is needed, per SS-4.

fields.name: start_date
fields.label: Date business started or acquired
fields.help: The date the entity began or was acquired.

fields.name: principal_activity
fields.label: Principal business activity
fields.help: Short description of what the business does.

fields.name: expects_employees
fields.label: Expect to have employees in next 12 months?
fields.help: Affects employment-tax fields on the form.
